text.title: Google Ads Optimization Cycle Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the google-ads-optimization methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Bidding strategy MUST match conversion history: <30 conv/mo → manual CPC; 30-100 → maximize-conversions; ≥100 → target-CPA / target-ROAS.
rule.rationale: Smart bidding fails without enough conversion signal; mismatched strategy burns budget.

rule.statement: Weekly cadence MUST include negative-keyword sweep on Search Terms cost > $5.
rule.rationale: Search Term junk accumulates weekly; the sweep is the cheapest CPA lever.

rule.statement: Keywords with QS <5 MUST be flagged for ad-group split, ad copy rewrite, or landing-page realignment before any bid increase.
rule.rationale: Low QS multiplies effective CPC; fixing QS is cheaper than out-bidding.

rule.statement: Monthly cycle MUST rebalance budget across campaigns by ROAS bucket: top quartile +20%, middle hold, bottom -20% or pause.
rule.rationale: Static budgets across uneven ROAS leave money on the table.

rule.statement: Every optimization change MUST be logged (timestamp, change, rationale, expected impact, owner).
rule.rationale: Without change-log, learning-phase resets stack invisibly.
